dimensions.name: Pedagogical Clarity
dimensions.description: How clearly learning objectives are communicated

scoreLevels.1-3: Objectives unclear, content confusing, no logical structure
scoreLevels.4-6: Objectives present but could be clearer, some structure issues
scoreLevels.9-10: Exceptionally clear, scaffolded perfectly, memorable delivery

scoreLevels.1-3: Dry, monotonous, likely to lose attention quickly
scoreLevels.4-6: Adequate but could be more engaging, some flat sections
scoreLevels.7-8: Engaging throughout, good use of examples and scenarios
scoreLevels.9-10: Compelling, uses storytelling, learners would want to continue

dimensions.name: Content Accuracy
dimensions.description: Is the information factually correct and complete?

scoreLevels.1-3: Contains errors, misleading, or significantly incomplete
scoreLevels.4-6: Mostly accurate but missing key details or nuances
scoreLevels.7-8: Accurate and comprehensive, covers all key points
scoreLevels.9-10: Impeccable accuracy, includes nuances and edge cases

dimensions.name: Pacing & Duration
dimensions.description: Right amount of information per scene, appropriate timing

scoreLevels.1-3: Way too fast or too slow, scenes wildly unbalanced
scoreLevels.4-6: Mostly okay but some scenes feel rushed or padded
scoreLevels.7-8: Well-paced, each scene has the right density
scoreLevels.9-10: Perfect pacing, natural breathing room, no rush or drag

dimensions.name: Visual Cue Integration
dimensions.description: Clear direction for downstream image/video production

scoreLevels.1-3: No visual direction, impossible to create matching visuals
scoreLevels.4-6: Some visual cues but vague or inconsistent
scoreLevels.7-8: Clear visual descriptions that map to narration
scoreLevels.9-10: Vivid, specific visual direction that enhances the narration
